argument_quality_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no assessment; score is neutral.
argument_quality_notes.landscape: Arguments were sound and correctly identified the generic nature of the landscape evidence, leading to a logical revision.
argument_quality_notes.botanics: Arguments were sound and correctly identified the non-diagnostic nature of the flora, leading to a logical revision.
argument_quality_notes.regulatory: Arguments were precise, citing specific visual evidence (hazard markings, wall quality) and effectively countering the generic arguments of other agents.
argument_quality_notes.meta: Agent provided no assessment; score is neutral.

revision_justification_notes.landscape: Revision was well-justified, accepting the superior diagnostic value of regulatory evidence over generic landscape features.
revision_justification_notes.botanics: Revision was well-justified, explicitly conceding that botanical evidence was non-diagnostic.
revision_justification_notes.regulatory: Maintained position effectively, reinforcing the strength of regulatory evidence.

debate_contribution_notes.landscape: Reiterated R1 points but framed them as non-diagnostic, facilitating the revision.
debate_contribution_notes.botanics: Reiterated R1 points but framed them as non-diagnostic, facilitating the revision.
debate_contribution_notes.regulatory: Effectively synthesized the visual evidence to override the dissenting agents.

verdict.moderator_pairing_notes: The moderator correctly prioritized the Regulatory agent (hard constraints) against the dissenting agents (Landscape and Botanics) to resolve the disagreement. This aligns perfectly with the pairing priority rules.
verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly followed the evidence hierarchy, prioritizing the concessions made in the debate (where agents admitted their evidence was non-diagnostic) and the hard constraints provided by the Regulatory agent.
